# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder_30b)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash

# Create temporary file with predictable name
TMP_FILE="/tmp/myapp-$$.tmp"

# Write data to temp file
echo "Processing data line 1" > "$TMP_FILE"
echo "Processing data line 2" >> "$TMP_FILE"
echo "Processing data line 3" >> "$TMP_FILE"

# Process the data (example: count lines)
LINE_COUNT=$(wc -l < "$TMP_FILE")

# Read results from same temp file
echo "Total lines processed: $LINE_COUNT"

# Process again with different operation (example: uppercase)
tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]' < "$TMP_FILE" > "${TMP_FILE}.upper"

# Read the uppercased results
echo "Uppercased output:"
cat "${TMP_FILE}.upper"

# Clean up temporary files
rm -f "$TMP_FILE" "${TMP_FILE}.upper"
